text: Ki Yisa nan u poorpoweiteeb din fiir ki sik mɔkgbeŋŋir na kpiŋ, ki niburchiauŋ din waa u poor po, binba din nyii Galilee nan Judea

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Sɔɔ kan fit kɔɔ paŋ daanɔ ŋaak ni ki saa jan u mɔkinti, see ki u dɔŋ ki lorɔ ŋaan yen ji fit biir u ŋaak maŋ.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa yaan din piin ki want niib Yennu maan Galilee mɔkgbeŋŋir kpiŋ, ki niburchiauŋ baar u boor ni. Wa din la ki nigbanu na yabit na, ki u kɔɔ burgbetsiar ni, ki sik nyun ni, ŋaan ki niib na biar ki see gbingbann.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Linba na poor po ki Yisa boib a “Ti daa sa bikin Yennu naan nan lannpoe? Ti sa yin barjouk lanne ki lin yent niib nunii po?

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Ki u bia boib a, “Sɔɔ mi ki kɔɔ nan fitir ŋaak ni ki chibin ŋmaŋ li paak, amii ki gurir gado lɔuŋ ni-ii? Aaii. See ki u jiir ki tɔɔn bonsiar nba fɔk paak.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Tiŋ nee tuu te ki bonboorii maŋ-n pa ki kpaatir ki bia naan, nyakirii tuu nyi sinsinn, li poor po ki faat-n nyi, ki dipukaa-n nyi ki wei.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ki Yisa nan u poorpoweiteeb poot Galilee mɔkgbeŋŋir na gbingbanleer po, Gerasa tingbouŋ ni.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Wa chab niib maŋ gbenn yoonba ki u doo jɔsiar paak ki mei Yennu.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Ki Farisiinba nan sennu wannteeb maŋ boi Yisa a “Bee ki a poorpoweiteeb na ki dia ti damm kaar nba ki ti yejamm din wannit na, ŋaan kpan gbia bi di jeet ki ki nit bi nii?”
